original_hypothesis.title: Artist Royalty Burn via Premature Admin Approval
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: An admin can call acceptAddressesAndPercentages before the artist has proposed any addresses, setting their status to true. Subsequent calls to payArtist will permanently burn the artist's ETH by sending it to address(0).
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Lack of validation on admin inputs allows operational mistakes to permanently destroy user funds.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Sets status to true without verifying if addresses are non-zero

preconditions: Admin calls acceptAddressesAndPercentages before artist proposes

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Admin can force approval of empty state

operational_questions: Is there off-chain tooling that prevents admins from doing this?
Why doesn't acceptAddressesAndPercentages validate the addresses?
validation_requirements: Test calling acceptAddressesAndPercentages on a fresh collection, then call payArtist

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Admin accidentally or maliciously calls acceptAddressesAndPercentages for a collection
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: The status flags are set to true, bypassing the artist proposal requirement
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: payArtist is called, calculating payouts based on 0 percentages and address(0)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: ETH is sent to address(0) or locked as dust, permanently burning the royalties

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: ETH sent to address(0) during payArtist

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
